---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Engine flag: TRUE only when this template is stitched into the
  # combined report (set by the parent). Leave false for normal renders.
  combined_mode: false
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_micro_survey.csv")
  
  # Variables
  category_var: "attrition_status"
  
  # Array Math
  scale_factor: 50   # 1 dot = X units. Adjust this so the total number of dots on screen is around 100.
  
  # Policy-Facing Labels
  unit_name: "Households"
  metric_name: "Survey Completion & Attrition"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Sample Attrition: Icon Array"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = !isTRUE(params$combined_mode),
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 6, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis', eval=!isTRUE(params$combined_mode)}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)

source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
# 1. Calculate the raw counts directly from the microdata
array_data <- raw_data %>%
  # Drop missing statuses
  drop_na(!!sym(params$category_var)) %>%
  
  # Scrub text and group messy data into strict attrition categories
  mutate(
    Clean_Status = str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$category_var))),
    Category = case_when(
      str_detect(Clean_Status, "(?i)Survey|Complete|Success") ~ "Surveyed",
      str_detect(Clean_Status, "(?i)Refuse|Deny") ~ "Refused",
      str_detect(Clean_Status, "(?i)Not Reach|Unreach|Miss|Absent") ~ "Not Reached",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  
  # Aggregate the counts
  count(Category, name = "count") %>%
  
  # 2. Calculate the number of dots needed based on the YAML scale factor
  mutate(dots_needed = round(count / params$scale_factor)) %>%
  # Drop categories that round down to 0 dots
  filter(dots_needed > 0)

# 3. "Uncount" the data: Copies each row N times so we have exactly one row per dot
dot_df <- array_data %>%
  uncount(dots_needed) %>%
  mutate(id = row_number())

# 4. Calculate grid dimensions dynamically to make it a rough square
total_dots <- nrow(dot_df)
cols <- ceiling(sqrt(total_dots))

# 5. Assign X and Y coordinates to construct the neat grid
dot_df <- dot_df %>%
  mutate(
    x = (id - 1) %% cols + 1,
    y = ceiling(id / cols),
    # Lock the factor levels so the most important categories plot and color correctly
    Category = factor(Category, levels = c("Surveyed", "Refused", "Not Reached", "Other"))
  )
```


```{r plot-icon-array}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

ggplot(dot_df, aes(x = x, y = y, color = Category)) +
  
  # Draw the "Icons" (thick circles). 
  # Using shape 16 (solid dot) with size 5 creates a clean, readable dot matrix.
  geom_point(size = 6, shape = 16, alpha = 0.9) +
  
  # coord_equal prevents the dots from stretching into ovals
  coord_equal() +
  
  # Use high-contrast colors to differentiate success from attrition
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Surveyed"    = unname(cerp_cols["primary"]),  # Dark Blue/CERP Primary
    "Refused"     = "#E69F00",                     # Orange/Warning
    "Not Reached" = unname(cerp_cols["neutral"]),  # Grey
    "Other"       = "#B0B0B0"                      # Light Grey
  )) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  # Strip all axes, gridlines, and backgrounds to leave only the dots floating in space
  theme(
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else (params$metric_name),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else (paste0("Each dot represents ", params$scale_factor, " ", tolower(params$unit_name), ".")),
    caption = params$source_note
  )
```
